mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals rising capacity utilization and corporate earnings strength, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change on 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive at 10% level over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 5%, further tightening compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return is not negative (or not significantly lower than the unconditional mean) during periods when FEDFUNDS is above 5% (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of falling unemployment and rising inflation signals an overheating economy that forces the Fed to tighten policy aggressively, which pushes equity prices lower over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (UNRATE monthly decline × CPIAUCSL monthly increase) is not negatively correlated with 20-day forward returns at rank IC < -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During the early phase of a monetary easing cycle, rising industrial production is a powerful signal that accommodative policy is successfully stimulating real activity, leading to above-average S&P 500 gains over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: early monetary easing (first 6 months after a rate cut)
failure_condition: Correlation between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns is not significantly higher in periods following a FEDFUNDS rate cut compared to all other periods (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Large monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls signal robust labor demand and rising household income, which boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, lifting S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return after positive payroll surprises >200k is not positive, or the proportion of positive returns does not exceed 65% (the unconditional positive rate).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong real GDP growth and stable to modest inflation (near 2%) defines a goldilocks environment where corporate earnings expand without discount rate headwinds, reliably producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: goldilocks economy (GDP growth > 3% y/y and CPI < 3% y/y)
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 70% of quarters classified as goldilocks, or the mean return in such quarters is not statistically greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which tends to lift equity valuations particularly in a low interest rate environment where mortgage rates remain subdued and housing affordability supports economic momentum.
regime_hint: low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below its historical median)
failure_condition: The positive association between HOUST changes and 20-day forward returns is not significantly stronger in low-FEDFUNDS regimes than in high-FEDFUNDS regimes (difference in rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in the Consumer Price Index erode real wages and squeeze corporate profit margins, while also prompting expectations of future monetary tightening, both of which depress equity prices over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of CPIAUCSL month-over-month change on 5-day forward returns is not negative and statistically significant at the 10% level over any 3-year rolling period.
